name: The Jurassic Park/world franchise
description:
🦖 Tyrannosaurus rex

Tyrannosaurus rex

The iconic “T. rex” of the franchise, often nicknamed Rexy, is a massive apex predator and one of the most important recurring dinosaurs. In Jurassic Park, she escapes containment and becomes both a terrifying force and an unexpected protector. Across the series, she is shown as powerful, intelligent, and surprisingly resilient, often stepping in during climactic battles against other predators like the Indominus rex.

🦖 Velociraptor (Blue and pack)

Velociraptor

In the franchise, Velociraptors are highly intelligent, social hunters capable of problem-solving and communication. They are trained (to a degree) by characters like Owen Grady in Jurassic World. Blue, the most famous raptor, shows loyalty, emotional bonding, and moral complexity—something not seen in real-world animal behavior but central to the films’ storytelling.

🦕 Brachiosaurus

Brachiosaurus

One of the first dinosaurs ever seen in Jurassic Park (1993), the Brachiosaurus is a gentle giant herbivore. It is portrayed as peaceful, slow-moving, and awe-inspiring. It symbolizes wonder and the emotional “magic” of seeing dinosaurs brought back to life.

🦖 Spinosaurus

Spinosaurus

Featured prominently in Jurassic Park III, the Spinosaurus is depicted as an aggressive, territorial super-predator even larger and more dominant than the T. rex in that film. It becomes the main antagonist dinosaur, known for its strength, speed, and ability to defeat other large predators.

🦖 Indominus rex

Indominus rex

A genetically engineered hybrid created for Jurassic World, the Indominus rex is designed to be smarter, deadlier, and more unpredictable than any natural dinosaur. It displays camouflage ability, high intelligence, and extreme aggression, making it the central antagonist of the 2015 film.

🦖 Indoraptor

Indoraptor

Introduced in Jurassic World: Fallen Kingdom, the Indoraptor is a smaller but highly lethal hybrid combining traits from the Indominus rex and Velociraptor. It is trained for military use, exhibits stalking behavior, and shows eerie intelligence and psychological hunting tactics.

🐊 Mosasaurus

Mosasaurus

A massive aquatic predator featured in Jurassic World, the Mosasaurus is kept in a lagoon and used as a live attraction. It is portrayed as one of the largest and most powerful creatures in the franchise, capable of devastating surface prey with explosive force.

🦖 Triceratops

Triceratops

A heavily built herbivore known for its three facial horns and large frill. In the franchise, Triceratops is usually portrayed as powerful but calm, though capable of intense defense when threatened. One of the most memorable early moments in Jurassic Park shows a sick Triceratops being
